cards.back: Java Server Pages (JSP) is a server-side scripting technology that allows developers to create web pages with dynamic content using Java code.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the Service class in a JSP?
cards.back: The Service class in a JSP is used to organize and encapsulate the business logic of a JSP, allowing developers to separate presentation and logic.

cards.front: What is the difference between JSP and Servlet?
cards.back: JSP is a markup language that compiles to Servlet code, whereas Servlet is the actual Java code executed by the server, and JSP is used to generate Servlet code at compile-time.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the directive directive in a JSP?
cards.back: The directive directive in a JSP is used to include other JSP files, import Java classes, and access servlets and other resources.

cards.front: What happens when a JSP file is compiled?
cards.back: When a JSP file is compiled, the Java code within it is compiled to Servlet code, which is then executed by the server to generate the dynamic content of the web page.
